pattern: The pairing condition only ever depends on two single digits pulled out of each number, so counting pairs reduces to a tiny fixed alphabet instead of the numbers themselves.
hints: You never actually need the full numbers -- only the first digit of the left one and the last digit of the right one ever enter the coprimality check.
There are only 9 possible first digits and 10 possible last digits, so a small running tally of how many first digits have been seen so far is enough to answer each new position quickly.

Walk the array once, treating each position as the 'later' element of a pair, and add up how many earlier first digits are coprime with this element's last digit before recording this element's own first digit for later positions.

edge_inputs.description: two elements, one pair, not coprime
multi-digit numbers with repeated first/last digits
maximum length, all pairs coprime (all firsts and lasts equal 1)
digit 1 is coprime with everything, easy to under-count
even first digits paired with even last digits never coprime

stress.description: 100 random valid numbers (no trailing zero digit)
